def: s.f. = gandharvī.
s.f. A princess of the Gāndhāris (esp. the wife of Dhr̤ita-rāshṭra); — one of the tutelary female deities of the Jains.

s.m. = gandharb, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), An aërial minstrel, a kind of celestial musician; a class or (one of a class) of demi-gods who inhabit the heaven of Indra and constitute the orchestra at all the banquets of the principal deities; — the soul after death and previous to its being born again (according to Hindū notions); — a horse; — a kind of deer
subs: s.m. or gandharb-gīt, s.m. The minstrelsy of the celestial choir; — a Gandharb's song
or gandharb-biwāh, or gandharb-byāh, s.m. Marriage from mutual agreement (before witnesses or in writing, and without the usual ceremonies).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Belonging or relating to the Gandharvas; — musical; — a form of marriage which requires only mutual agreement; — the art of the Gandharvas, song, music, dance.

s.m. (f. -ī), An aërial minstrel, a kind of celestial musician; a class or (one of a class) of demi-gods who inhabit the heaven of Indra and constitute the orchestra at all the banquets of the principal deities; — the soul after death and previous to its being born again (according to Hindū notions); — a horse; — a kind of deer
subs: s.m. or gandharb-gīt, s.m. The minstrelsy of the celestial choir; — a Gandharb's song
or gandharb-biwāh, or gandharb-byāh, s.m. Marriage from mutual agreement (before witnesses or in writing, and without the usual ceremonies).

def: fem. of gandharv, q.v.
s.f. The plant 'Lemon grass,' Andropogon schœnanthus (also written gandh-bel, q.v.).
a surname, Gandhi, originating as an occupation; Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi (1869–1948)
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi گاندھی (gandhy /⁠gāndhī⁠/) (c. 1654), from Apabhramsa गंधीय (gaṃdhīya), from Prakrit 𑀕𑀁𑀥𑀺𑀅 (gaṃdhia), from Sanskrit गान्धिक (gāndhika). Proper noun sense 1.1 is a semantic loan from Gujarati ગાંધી (gāndhī).

def: s.m. (f. -inī), A seller of perfumes (as rose-water, ʻat̤r, &c.); a perfumer; — a bug; — the green flying-bug.
more: s. m. 1. A person who sells rosewater, essences, and aﻌtr; a perfumer. 2. A green bug.

def: s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), A vendor of perfumes, a perfumer; — a kind of worm having a strong fœtid smell, a tree-bug.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Having a smell (whether good or bad); scented, odorous, fragrant; stinking, fœtid; — a scented person, &c.
s.m. Name of a vagrant tribe (prob. the same as gaṅḍhū).
s.f. An epithet of the Ganges or of the goddess Gangā ( = gāndinī).
n. m. S. गन्धसग 1. Gardenia florida . 2. Sandalwood.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Jāṭs.
s.f. Name of a princess of Kāśi (who was the wife of Śvaphalka and mother of Akrūra; — see gāndinī).
s.m. = gandhī, q.v. s.m. (f. -inī), A seller of perfumes (as rose-water, ʻat̤r, &c.); a perfumer; — a bug; — the green flying-bug.
more: n. m. gandan , S. गान्धिक, Pālī, gaṅdhiko . A perfumer.

def: s.m. = gaṇḍ-mālā, q.v.s.v. gaṇḍ. s.f. = گانڙ gāṅṛ, q.v. s.f. The anus; the privities
subs: (-kī), To get into a funk, to be in a great fright

def: s.f. A circle (esp. that which Rāma drew round Sītā to protect her from Rāvaṇa; — (dialec.) bank or mound (round a tank).
more: s. A circle, particularly that which Rāmachandra drew round Sītā to protect her from Rāvaṇa. See under رام چندر.

def: s.f. A stinking wench (a term of abuse); a coarse, dirty woman.
s.m. A pathic, catamite; a poltroon; an impotent fellow; a term of abuse (without any particular implication); — adj. Impotent, cowardly (cf. gāṅḍū).
s.m. = gaṅḍhīlā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Having a smell (whether good or bad); scented, odorous, fragrant; stinking, fœtid; — a scented person, &c.
s.f. 1˚ = gāndī; — 2˚ = gāndhinī, q.v. s.f. Name of a princess of Kāśi (who was the wife of Śvaphalka and mother of Akrūra; — see gāndinī).
s.m. A bow; the bow of Arjun.
wety: From گانْڈ (gānḍ) + ـُو (-ū).

def: s.m. A bow; the bow of Arjun.
more: s. m. The name of the bow of Arjun.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Armed with a bow; — an epithet of Arjun.
s.m. A pathic, catamite; a poltroon; an impotent fellow; a term of abuse (without any particular implication); — adj. Impotent, cowardly (cf. gāṅḍū).
more: s. (dakh.) A catamite ( Harris ): see گانڐو gānḍ-ū.

def: s.f. See s.v. gaṇḍ. s.f. = گانڙ gāṅṛ, q.v. s.f. The anus; the privities
subs: (-kī), To get into a funk, to be in a great fright

def: n. m. Inflammation of the glands of the neck; the goitre of the Alps; scrofulā; the king's evil.
Bhoj. The place where the cut sugarcane is collected for pressing.
s.m. A catamite, a sodomite; — an impotent man; a timid person, a coward.
more: s. m. A catamite, a sodomite. (Cinædus).
wety: From گانْڈ (gānḍ) + ـُو (-ū).

def: n. m. 1. A catamite. 1. Pānīpat, Karnāl, bārah baras kā gāṅḍū, terah baras kī chhināl . Prov. At twelve a catamite, at thirteen wantons quite, Karnal and Panipat ill-famed! 2. Damṛī kī haṅṛiyā, dulhā sāṛo ganṛiyā . Boys. A farthing's worth the cooking pot, the bridegroom a catamite every jot. 3. Phaṅse kyoṅkar na ā-kar in se laṛkā gul-badan, gul-rū. Garaz lauṅḍā to kyā, yeh to buḍḍhe ko kareṅ gāṅḍū, Phireṅge yeh līye aisā agar bachcha gilahrī kā . Nazīr. No wonder snared are in his toils each tender youth, Put boys aside, old men he'd catamites make in sooth If he with squirrels goes about like this for sooth. 2. An impotent. 3. A coward; a timid person. Gāṅḍū kā himāëtī bhī hārā hai . Prov. For the coward's second but defeat.
s.m. Wheat
more: Wheat. Gandum az gandum biroyad, jau zi jau . P. Prov. From wheat wheat grows, from barley barley. (The son is like the father). gandum-raṅg, gandum-gūṅ, gandumī ; Tir. gahū- mīraṅg , adj. Brownish yellow; tawny.
s. m. Wheat. گندم کرماني gandum-i-kirmānī, Vermicelli. گندم گون gandum-gūn, or گندم رنگ gandum-rang, adj. Swarthy, brown, russet.
subs: or gandum-rang, adj. Wheat-coloured, tawny, brown, swarthy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گندم (gandom). Doublet of گیہُوں (gehū̃).

def: adj. = gandum-rang, q.v.s.v. gandum.
ex:
جب کہ ایسا ہو گندمی لونڈا
تب گنہگار کیوں نہ ہو آدم
jab ki aisā ho gandumī lauṇḍā
tab gunahgār kyõ na ho ādam

when there is such a wheat-complexioned boy
why then should not Adam be a sinner?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گندمی (gandumī), from گندم (gandum, “wheat”).

def: direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
s.f. A kind of long grass used for thatching (cf. ganaurī).
s.m. See s.v. gaṇ. s.m. A heavy stone for pressing oil, &c.; a stone or wooden mortar; — (in Urdū), adj. Dead drunk.
more: The name of a Hindū divinity. The Janus of the Hindūs. The god of wisdom, represented with an elephantʼs head and attended by a rat. He was the son of Mahādeva and of a daughter of the mountain Himālaya, and is invoked at the commencement of every affair of consequence.

def: s.m. See s.v. gaṇ. s.m. A heavy stone for pressing oil, &c.; a stone or wooden mortar; — (in Urdū), adj. Dead drunk.
more: n. m. The Hin. god of wisdom. He is represented with an elephant's head and four hands, and attended by a rat which is his vehicle. He is the patron of students and shopkeepers, and is invoked ( ganesh pūjā ) at the commencement of every affair of consequence. 1. Jaë Ganesh! jaë Ganesh! jaë Ganesh Devā! Māt vākī Pārbatī, pitā Mahādevā! Pān chaṛhe, phūl chaṛhe, aur chaṛhe mevā, Laḍḍuoṅ kā bhog lage, su-phal terī sevā . Glory, glory, glory, to the god Ganesh e'er be! To Mahadeo his sire, and his mother Parbati! Offer pan and offer flowers, pile with fruits his fane, Sweetmeats too, and trust your service will not be in vain. 2. Chauth karūṅgī bart Ganesh, jo kātūṅ to hoë kalesh . Wom. song. The fourth day for Ganesh a fast I keep, To spin this day I should have cause to weep. ganesh-chauth , n. A. Hindu festival observed in honor of Ganesh on the 4th Aghan. gobar-ganesh , n. lit . the imag…
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit गणेश (gaṇeśa).

ety: S. गङ्गा; Pers. also gang, m.
def: s.f. The Ganges; — a river, stream
more: s. m. Name of a river. See گنگا.
s. f. A river, a stream. دوگنگ do-gang, In two streams (a river).
subs: or gang-bar-āmad, s.f. Alluvial land recovered from a river (esp. from the Ganges)
s.f. Encroachment of the Ganges (or of any other river) by diluvion

def: s.f. The river Ganges (there is also a Ganges in the sky — ākāś-gaṅgā — and one under the earth); — a river or stream (generally)
more: n. f. from गम् to go. 1. The river Ganges. 2. The goddess. 1. Jahāṅ Gaṅg, vahāṅ raṅg . Prov. Where Ganges flows there gala shows. 2. Āge tere Gaṅgā, pīchhe tere Gayā . An oath and a jest. The holy Ganges before you, and sacred Gaya at your back. 3. Chakkī phoṛūṅ, chūlhā phoṛāṅ, ghar ko āg lagāūṅgī, Chāle hai to chāl, nigoṛe! maiṅ to Gaṅgā nhāūṅgī . Wom. song. I'll break the mill, the oven smash and fire my house and home But I will in the Ganges bathe, come, wretch if you 're to come! 4. Gaṅgā nhā-kar kyā phal pāë, mūṅchh muṅḍā-kar ghar ko āë . Boys. In Ganges bathed what is your gain, your lips clean shaved you're home again. 5. Gangā nhāë mukat hoë to meṅḍak, machchhiyāṅ, Mūṅḍ munḍāë sidh hoë to bheṛ kapaṭṭiyāṅ . Kabir. If Ganges cleanse from sin how pure must frogs and fishes be! If tonsure's holiness thrice blessed the sheep you fleece must be! gaṅgā ashnān, gaṅgā nahān , n. m. 1. Ba…
subs: (for gaṅgā-vāsī), s.m. One who resides near the Ganges
s.m. The further bank of the Ganges, or of any river; the country beyond the Ganges
s.m. A Brāhmaṇ who conducts pilgrims to the Ganges; a Brāhmaṇ priest who officiates by the Ganges; — a man of low caste employed in removing dead bodies
s.f. Dying on the banks of the Ganges
s.m. Bank of the Ganges; — country along the banks of the Ganges
s.f. A vessel in which Ganges-water is kept
'To take up a vessel containing Ganges-water,' to swear on the water of the Ganges

s.f. The Ganges and Jamnā rivers; — a mixture of any kind; — a mode of adjusting the interest of a loan (the debtor being credited with the interest of the instalments paid by him, while he is debited, per contra, with the interest of the full amount on the original loan)

adj. Mixed (as oil and butter); made of mixed metal (as of gold and silver, or of brass and copper); — s.f. A mixture of gold and silver, or of brass and copper; — an ear-ring made of such mixed metal
(ʻthe gate of the Gangeś), s.m. The place (Hardwār) where the Ganges issues from the mountains and enters the plains

s.m. Ganges-supporter'; an epithet of Śiva (according to the legend, the Ganges, in its descent from heaven, first alighted on the head of Śiva, and continued for a long period entangled in his hair); a proper name (of a man)
s.m. The place where the Ganges empties itself into the ocean; the mouths of the Ganges
vulg. gaṅgā-asnān or aśnān, or gaṅgā-nahān, s.m. Bathing in the Ganges (esp. as an act of devotion); — an annual fair held on the Ganges on the last day of Kārttik
s.m. The sacred district of the Ganges, i.e. the river Ganges and the country extending about two kos on either side of its banks (all dying within these limits go to heaven, whatever their crimes)
To die on either bank of the Ganges; to go to heaven
To bathe in the Ganges; to be freed from sin and go to heaven
(˚gā+ut˚), s.f. The source of the Ganges, the place where it springs from the mountains
(˚gā+ud˚), s.m. Ganges water; — water of any sacred river
(˚gā+ūr˚), s.m. The rising of the Ganges (or of any other river) in the rains
s.f. Pilgrimage to, the Ganges; — carrying a sick person to the river-side to die there.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गङ्गा (gaṅgā).

def: the holy water of the Ganges
n. m. lit . the source of the Ganges. An epithet of Siva.
more: s. m. An epithet of शिव Siva.

def: s. f. A kind of earring.
s.m. A large metallic vessel for holding water.
s.m. Land subject to inundations of the Ganges.
n. m. Lands subject to inundations of the Ganges. gaṅgāl' , n. m. A large metallic vessel for holding water. ganglā shalgam , Large turnips grown on the Ganges; renunculus acris , Wat.
s.f. A blind boil.
more: n. f. A blind boil. Gāṅgan mātā ke ghī, pān bāṅdho .
s. m. The name of a disorder.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = gargaiyā = gauraiyā, q.v.
s.f. Ganges-sand.
Ganges' sand.
n. f. The source of the Ganges.

s.m. corr. of gāhak, q.v. s.m. (f. -ikā), Taker; seizer (e.g. jān-kā gāhak, 'one who takes or aims at life'); receiver, getter; accepter; approver, appreciator (e.g. guṇ-gāhak); — purchaser, buyer, chapman, vendor, dealer.
s.m. corr. of gehūṅ, q.v. s.m. Wheat ( = gohūṅ).
s.f. 'Gunny,' a strong coarse kind of sacking made from the fibres of Corchorus olitorius, and of C. capsularis.
wety: Borrowed from English guinea and Guinea.

def: adj. & s.m. Free from want; possessing a competence or sufficiency; independent (of); able to dispense (with); rich, wealthy, opulent; — an independent person; a rich man.
more: n. m. 1. A rich person. 2. An independent person. ganī , adj. Rich ( daulatmand ).
(part. act. of غنا) adj. 1. Independent. 2. Rich, wealthy, opulent.

def: s.f. Computation, calculation; enumeration; — estimate; judgment.
wety: Borrowed from English guinea and Guinea.

def: s.f. Computation, calculation; enumeration; — estimate; judgment.
wety: Borrowed from English guinea and Guinea.

ety: for A. غانٍ g̠āniṉ, act. part. of غني
def: adj. = g̠anī, q.v.
s.f. A harlot, courtezan; a dancing-girl; — a species of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum.
more: n. f. S. गै 1. A songstress; a dancing girl. 2. A courtezan; harlot. 3. Jasmine; Jasminum auriculatum . (?)

def: s.m. Taker of spoil ( = A. g̠ānim), plunderer; enemy, foe, adversary.
more: n. m. غنم took as spoil. An enemy.
(from غنم) s. m. An enemy, an adversary, a foe, a plunderer.

ety: for A. غنيمة, inf. n. of غنم 'to get, or take, as spoil,' &c.
def: s.f. Plunder, spoil, booty; a prize; a boon, blessing, a God-send; a piece of good luck, good fortune; abundance; convenience; accommodation.
more: n. f. 1. Abundance; plenty; heaps (اِکتفا). 2. A blessing. 1. Yeh bhī ganīmat hai . Even this much is enough. 2. Jo dam guzre so ganīmat hai . Prov. Saying. Life is blessing enough. ganīmat jānnā , v. a. To prize; esteem; value. Jo nikle so ganīmat jāno! All that's saved is gain.
(from غنم) s. f. 1. Plunder, a prize. 2. Abundance, good fortune, a blessing.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَنِیمَت (ġanīmat), from Arabic غَنِيمَة (ḡanīma).

def: part. (f. -ā), Counted, numbered, enumerated, reckoned, calculated; — s.f. (but neut. in S.) Reckoning, enumeration-calculation, computation; number; — the science of computation, arithmetic; mathematics
more: n. f. 1. ( ganit parkāsh ) Arithmetic. 2. ( ganit biddyā ) Mathematics. ganit-kār , n. m. 1. An arithmetician. 2. An astrologer.
s. f. Arithmetick, numeration. گنت کار गणितकार gaṇit-kār, s. m. An astrologer, arithmetician.
s. (dakh.) Calculation, numeration, number ( Harris ): see s. gaṇit. گنت کرنا ginnat karnā, To calculate.
subs: or gaṇit-kar, s.m. (f. -ī), An arithmetician; an astrologer
vidyā, vulg. gaṇit-bidyā, s.f. The science of calculation, mathematics.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गणित (gaṇita).

def: s.m. (in Pers., Urdū, and Hindī), A store, hoard; treasure; hidden treasure; a treasury; a magazine, granary, store-house, grain-market; an emporium for grain; a place of merchandize, a market, mart; a case (of instruments, or brushes, or razors, &c.); — a heap, pile, stack, rick (of grain, or hay, or wood, &c.); — (in Hindī) a mine; jewel-mine; jewel-room; place where plate, &c. is preserved; — a tavern; — disrespect, contempt
more: n. m. S. गञ्ज a mine. 1. A hoard; treasure. 2. A market; mart. 3. An emporium for grain. 4. A pile; stack; heap. ganj-i-shahīdāṅ , n. m. The burial place of martyrs; a catacomb. ganj-i-qārūṅ . The wealth of Crœsus.
n. m. A scald head; baldness; Tinea capitis. Chhalnī sir par na rakkho, ganj ho jāëgā . Wom. Dont put your sieve on your head, you 'll become bald! (A superstition).

s. m. 1. A granary, heap, treasure, treasury, hoard, market, mart. 2. A case. گنج بخش ganj-baḵẖsh, adj. Generous, liberal, profuse, extravagant. گنج گاو ganj-i-gāw, One of the great treasures of Jamshīd. گنجنامه ganj-nāma, Treasure-book.
subs: adj. Generous, liberal, lavish, profuse, extravagant
s.m. The burial-place of martyrs; a catacomb
s.m. One of the great treasures of Jamshed (it was dug up in the reign of Bahrām, and distributed amongst the poor)

def: s.m. Scald-head, baldness, Tinea capitis.

s.m. = gāṅjhā, q.v. s.m. The hemp-plant, Cannabis sativa; the leaves or young buds of the hemp-plant (the fructification, when nearly ripe, is bruised and smoked for intoxication: the dried leaves are ground in water, and drunk for the same purpose; in this state it is called bhaṅg, or sabzī)
more: s. m. (dakh.) The leaves or young leaf-buds of the hemp plant ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: (or gāṅje-wālā), s.m. A vendor of gāṅjā or intoxicating preparations of hemp.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀁𑀚 (gaṃja), from Sanskrit गञ्जा (gañjā).

def: adj. & s.m. = گنجا ganjā, q.v. s.m. = gāṅjhā, q.v. s.m. The hemp-plant, Cannabis sativa; the leaves or young buds of the hemp-plant (the fructification, when nearly ripe, is bruised and smoked for intoxication: the dried leaves are ground in water, and drunk for the same purpose; in this state it is called bhaṅg, or sabzī)
subs: (or gāṅje-wālā), s.m. A vendor of gāṅjā or intoxicating preparations of hemp.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Scald-headed; bald-headed, bald; — destroyed, effaced; — a scald-head, a bald-head.
more: adj. Bald; scald-headed. 1. Khudā ganje ko nākhūn na de! Prov. Heaven grant the bald-headed have no nails! 2. Andhā, ganjā, chug-ḍaṛhyā, aur kāṅṛā, Suno, bhaī sādho, in se mat patiyānā! Blind, bald-head, short-beard, and one-eyed, Let none, O man! in these confide! 3. Ganjī panhyārī, aur gokhrū kā īṅḍhvā! Prov. The water-bearer hath no hair on her head, and prickly gokhru for a pad she has spread.

def: s.m. Contempt; disrespectful treatment; contemptuous language; abuse.

ety: prob. for S. गन्धिनी; cf. gandhnī
def: s.f. A kind of aromatic grass, Andropogon schœnanthes (syn. gandh-bel, or gand-bel)
more: n. f. An aromatic grass; Andropogon schœnanthus .

def: s.f. A kind of grass or verdure.
more: s. f. A kind of grass or verdure.

def: s.m. = ganjhail, q.v. s.m.f. A person addicted to the use of gāṅjhā.
s.f. = ganjiyā, q.v. s.f. A wallet, a small bag; the coarse canvass bag of carters and grass-cutters.
s.m. = ganjhail, q.v. s.m.f. A person addicted to the use of gāṅjhā.

n. m. A smoker of hemp; one addicted to its use. Nār tīsrī yūṅ uṭh bolī, 'mile ganjeṛī mujhe piyā, Sau sau chilmeṅ gānjā pīte, nā jānūn kāhe kā hīyā .' Song. Lāonī. The third then said 'to a gānjā smoker I'm belayed, Some hundred pipes he smokes, God knows of what he's made.

s.m. The hemp-plant, Cannabis sativa; the leaves or young buds of the hemp-plant (the fructification, when nearly ripe, is bruised and smoked for intoxication: the dried leaves are ground in water, and drunk for the same purpose; in this state it is called bhaṅg, or sabzī)
more: n. m. S. गञ्जिका or गञ्जविका The hemp plant; Cannabis sativa . The leaves, called bhaṅg and sabzī , are intoxicating. 1. Lage gāṅje kā dam, aur miṭ jāë pal meṅ gam . (Gānjā smokers' prelude). When the gānjhā pipe begins to draw cares from the mind at once withdraw. 2. Gānjā piye, giyān baṛhe. Gānjā smokers. Ganjhā smoke, knowledge invoke! 3. Gāṅjā pīë gur-gayān ghaṭe, aur ghaṭe tan andar kā, Khokat khokat dam nikse, mukh dekho jaise bandar kā . Who gānjha smoke do knowledge lack, the heart burns constantly, The breath with coughing goes, the face as monkey's pale you see. gāṅje-vālā , n. m. A vendor of intoxicating preparations of hemp.

s. m. The hemp plant. (Cannabis sativa). The fructification, when nearly ripe, is bruised and smoked for intoxication. The leaves dried are ground in water and drunk for the same purpose. In this state it is called bhang and sabzī.
subs: (or gāṅje-wālā), s.m. A vendor of gāṅjā or intoxicating preparations of hemp.

def: s.m.f. A person addicted to the use of gāṅjhā.
more: adj. Addicted to the use of گانجها gānjhā, q. v.

def: s.f. (Dakh.), corr. of kānjī, q.v. s.f. A kind of sour gruel, or vinegar, made by steeping rice, or mustard-seed, &c. in water and letting the liquor ferment (it is said to create appetite and aid digestion); — water in which rice has been boiled, rice-gruel, 'congee'; — starch (prepared from water in which rice has been boiled)
more: s. f. (dakh.) Rice-gruel, commonly called by Europeans conjee ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.m. (colloq.) 'Congee-house,' a lock-up; a pound (for cattle).

def: s.m. = ganjīfa, q.v.

def: s.m. A pack of cards; — a game of cards
more: s. m. A pack of cards, a game of cards. گنجيفه ساز ganjīfa-sāz, Cardmaker.
subs: s.m. A card-player; — a knave, trickster, cheat

def: s.m. A treasury; magazine, &c. (see ganj).
more: s. m. A treasury, a magazine.

more: n. f. The coarse canvas bag of carters and grass cutters.
s. f. A wallet, a bag (in which grass-cutters keep their sickle, &c.).

more: E. v. a. P. گنج a granary. To store; hoard; heap.
v. a. 1. To store, to hoard. 2. To stir, to agitate. 3. To churn.

ety: prob. Prk. गज्जणअं; S. गर्जनीयं, rt. गर्ज्; or S. खर्जनीयं
def: v.t. To stir, agitate; to churn.
v.t. To treat with contempt; to treat disrespectfully; to abuse; — to destroy.
s.m.f. (dialec.) Addicted to smoking gāṅjhā; — an excessive smoker of gāṅjā.
s.m. A treasurer.
s.m. A mass of dough baked on embers; an inferior kind of bread made of arhar and other hard grains.

wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

or gāṅʼo-baṭ, s.f. Division of villages; division of a taʻalluqa into separate villages
or gāṅʼo-ḵẖarć, s.m. Village expenses or charges; municipal expenses

s.m. (f. -inī) = gāṅʼo-wālā, s.m. (f. -ī), A villager, a rustic.

def: v.n. & t. (Braj.) = gānā, q.v. s.f. Freedom from need or want; competence, sufficiency; richness, wealthiness; riches, wealth; — content.
villager
s.m. See s.v. gaṇ. s.m. A heavy stone for pressing oil, &c.; a stone or wooden mortar; — (in Urdū), adj. Dead drunk.
s.f. The anus; the privities

def: n. f. S. गुदा or गण्ड Pālī, gudam . 1. The anus . 1. Gāṅṛ na hotī to auliyā ho jāte . Slang. Prov. If he had n't a fundament he might have been a saint. 2. The bottom. 2. Ye to jānte hī nahīṅ ki ber kī gāṅṛ kidhar hai . Prov. He does n't even know the head from the tail. gāṅṛ phāṛnā , Slang, v. a. To put in a great fright. Jī chāhe bairāg ne, aur kunbā phāṛe gāṅḍ . Prov. gāṅṛ phaṭnā, gāṅṛ phaṭ-kar hauz yā bandūq honā , Slang. v. n. lit . to be cracked (the anus ). To be in a great fright. gāṅṛ-joṛ bakhiyā , Slang, n. f. Close friendship. gāṅṛ-chāṭan , n. m. Sucking one's bum. gāṅṛ chāṭnā , Slang, v. a. 1. To lick one's a—e. 2. To fawn and flatter. gāṅṛ chalnā , Slang, v. n. To be loose (the bowels). Gāṅṛ chale, man bakhtoṅ ko! Prov. His bowels loose, he wants parched gram! gāṅṛ dhonā , v. a. 1. To wash after evacuation. 2. To do menial offices for one. gāṅṛ ragaṛnā , Slang. v. n. To try or labor hard. gāṅr se ghoṛā kholnā, yā gul katarnā , Obscene Slang, v. a. To try one's best in vain. gāṅṛ gulāmī k. , Slang, v. a. To be slavish or subservient to one. gāṅṛ kāṭnā , v. a. To circumcise. (Said by women who are too modest to name the amputated member). gāṅṛ kī khabar, yā hosh na h., gāṅṛ galat h ., Slang. To be senseless; to know nothing. gāṅṛ gardan ek h. , Slang, v. n. 1. To be doubled up (with fatigue). 2. To be very fat. gānṛ gale meṅ ānā , Slang, v. n. To be entangled in one's own snares. gāṅṛ-ghisnī , ( Slang ) Labor in vain. gāṅṛ mārnā , v. a. Slang. To commit sodomy. gāṅṛ marānā , Slang, v. n. To suffer sodomy, as a catamite. gāṅṛ-marānī , A strumpet. (An abuse). gāṅṛ-maravval , Slang, n. f. Reciprocal sodomy. gāṅṛ-meṅ uṅglī k. , Slang, v. a. 1. To thrust one's finger up the fundament. 2. To worry; harass. gāṅṛ meṅ gū nā h. , v. n. Vul . To be extremely poor. gāṅṛ meṅ laṅgoṭī tak na h. , v. n. To be without a shred to cover one's nakedness. Gāṅṛ meṅ laṅgoṭī, na sir pe ṭopī . Prov. Not a rag to his bottom, nor a cap for his head. gaṅr-jhap , Slang, Going back from one's word; backing out. parāī gāṅṛ kā palītā , n. m. 1. A toad-eater; a lick-spittle. 2. One who is lavish at another's expense.

n. m. 1. A troop; tribe; flock; class; multitude. 2. An advanced guard. Simbhu gaṅṛ . 3. Inferior deities; demigods. 4. A series of lunar mansions classed under the three heads of gods, men, and demons. 5. ( Gram .) A series of roots or words belonging to the same rule and called after the first word of the series. 6. ( Pros .) A foot of four instants. 7. ( Arith .) A number.
s.m. (rustic) = gāṅḍā, q.v. s.m. Sugar-cane, &c. (i.q. gannā, q.v.). s.m. Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum; — (dialec.) a reed; a cane; reed-grass (rustic forms are gaṇḍā; gāṅḍā)
adj. & s.m. corr. of gāṇḍīv, q.v. s.m. A bow; the bow of Arjun.
s.m. A rustic corr. of ganḍāsā, q.v. s.m. A pole-axe; battle-axe; — an instrument (a kind of chopper) for cutting sugar-cane, jawāṛ-stalks, &c. for fodder.
s.m. = gāṅḍū, q.v. s.m. A catamite, a sodomite; — an impotent man; a timid person, a coward.
s.m. The angle or enclosed space at the junction of the fingers and of the toes.
more: W. n. m. The angle at the junction of the fingers and toes.

ety: gāṅs˚, q.v. + Prk. इआ = S. aff. इका
def: s.f. The iron head of an arrow or spear, &c.
more: n. f. S. अङ्कुश a hook. The iron head of an arrow.
s. f. The iron point of an arrow.

ety: prob. Prk. गसणअं; S. ग्रस नीयं, rt. ग्रस्
def: v.t. To take in, inclose, hem in, surround, encompass; — to pierce, transfix, spit (as a fowl, &c.); to bore, perforate; to string, thread
more: v. a. See ṭhāṅsnā . To pierce; transfix.
v. a. To pierce, transfix, spit (as, a fowl, &c.).

def: s.f. = H گانٿنا गांटना gāṅṭnā, v.t. dialec. forms of gāṅṭh, and gāṅṭhnā respectively, qq.v.
s.m. (Dakh.), corr. of ghanṭā, q.v. s.m. A bell; a plate of brass or of mixed metal for striking the hours on, a gong; — a clock; — an hour
subs: or ghaṇṭā denā, or ghaṇṭā mārnā, To ring a bell; to strike a gong

s.m. One who has bell-like ears; one whose ears are decorated with bells; — name of an attendant of Skanda; — one of Śiva's attendants (he is supposed to preside over cutaneous complamts, and is worshipped for exemption from them in the month Ćaitra); — name of a Piśāća attendant on Kuvera

def: s. (dakh) An hour ( Harris ).
s.m. (Dakh.) = ganṭham, q.v. s.m. An iron style (for writing on palm-leaves).

s.f. A knot, tie, fastening; bond, engagement, stipulation; a knot or knob (in wood); a joint, articulation (as of a reed, or of the body, or of the muscles, &c.); a hardened or enlarged gland; a kernel; the navel; — a bundle, parcel, bale (of goods); a bunch; — a purse; a pocket; — entanglement, difficulty; misunderstanding; offence, displeasure; — dissimulation, guile
more: s. f. 1. A knot; joint, an articulation. 2. A bundle. گانٿهہ پڙنا gānṭh paṛnā, is applied to the fixing of enmity towards any one in the breast. گانٿهہ دار gānṭh-dār, adj. Knotted. گانٿهہ کا پورا gānṭh kā pūrā, Rich, wealthy. گانٿهہ کا کهونا gānṭh kā khonā, To act to oneʼs own prejudice. گانٿهہ کهولنا gānṭh kholnā, 1. The untying of a knot. 2. Prodigality, expense, to open the purse. 3. Removal of prejudice. گانٿهہ گٿهيلا gānṭh-gaṭhīlā, Knotty (as, a stick), compact (as, a man).
subs: or gāṅṭh bandhan karnā (-meṅ), To tie a knot (in, a handkerchief, &c., by way of a reminder); to tie together; to make an engagement or contract (with a person, for, -kī, a thing); to harbour prejudice, &c. (e.g. man-kī gāṅṭh bāṅdhnā)
adj. (f. -ī), Full of knots, knotty; intricate; — having full pockets, or having the pocket full of money
(-meṅ), To become knotted or entangled; (met.) misunderstanding or ill-feeling to arise; to harbour inveterate enmity (towards anyone, in the breast)
(-kī), To tie the knot (of), to splice; to tie the nuptial knot
To have a rupture at the navel
(-meṅ), To tie a knot (in, a string, or a handkerchief, for the purpose of reminding one of anything)
To be lost (a thing); to suffer loss
The navel to slip out of its place; to suffer from umbilical hernia
adj. (f. -ī), One's own purse, &c.; one's own; private
adj. & s.m. Having a full purse, wealthy; — a wealthy person
To waste or squander (one's) money; — to act to (one's) prejudice, to do oneself an injury, to incur loss
(-kī), To have (one's) purse cut off or stolen, to have (one's) pocket picked, to be robbed
v.t. To put in one's purse or pocket; to hoard up; — to pocket, to misappropriate

(-kī), To untie a knot or knots; to disentangle; — to open (one's) purse; to spend; — to remove prejudice, or misunderstanding, or ill-feeling; — s.m. The untying a knot; — opening the purse-strings, prodigality, extravagance; expense; — removal of prejudice, &c.
adj. (f. -ī), Knotted; knotty (as a stick); compact (as a man)
v.t. To put in (one's) purse or pocket, to pocket

def: s.f. = gaṭh, q.v.
subs: s.m., and gaṅṭh-joṛā, s.m. See s.v. gaṭh.

def: n. f. S. ग्रन्यि Pālī, ganṭho . See عقده and آنتَ (1, 3.) 1. A knot; tie. 2. A joint; an articulation. 3. A hardened or enlarged gland. 4. ( gāṅṭheṅ ) An obstruction in the stomach ( suddah ). Peṭ meṅ gāṅṭheṅ haiṅ! 5. The navel. 6. A pocket; purse. 1. Khoṭe dām gāṅṭh līye ḍole, maṅhgī bastu mulāve . Kabīr. False coin he in his pocket takes, for precious goods he bargains makes. 2. Gāṅṭh girah meṅ kauṛī nahīṅ, Bāṅkepur kī sair! Prov. In his pocket not a sou, gay dames of Bankipur he goes to woo! 3. Gāṅṭh girah meṅ kauṛī nahīṅ, mīyān gaṭṭe-vāle hot! Prov. About him not a sou, ho! sweetmeat seller, ho! 4. Gāṅṭh na muṭthī, phar pharāë uṭṭhī . Prov. Empty pockets, not a fistful, wares for sale—up jumps she wistful. 6. A bundle; parcel; bale of goods. 7. A piece of turmeric, etc. Haldī yā soṅṭh kī gāṅṭh . 8. A difficulty. 9. A misunderstanding. 10. A stipulation. 11. Dissimulation; guile. Sādhū vohī sarāhiye jāke hirde gāṅṭh, Laḍḍū le bhītar dhare, charnāmat de bāṅṭ . On wily priest for choice your praises lavished be, Who offerings hoards, but deals out idol washings free. gāṅṭh yā girah bāṅdhnā, denā, lagānā, yā mārnā; gāṅṭh bandhan k. , v. a. 1. To tie a knot in one's handkerchief; to bear in mind. 2. To tie together. 3. To make a contract. gāṅṭh-bharā , adj. 1. Knotty; intricate. 2. Full (pockets); flush (of cash). gāṅṭh paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be knotted. 2. To arise (misunderstanding, ill-feeling). 3. To be established (a soreness, raw, etc.). Mere piyā ke jīyā meṅ paṛī hai gāṅṭh, maiṅ kaun jatan se kholūṅ? Song. Thumrī . In my love's mind a knot I find, how now can I this knot untie? gānṭh joṛnā , v. a. To tie the nuptial knot. gāṅṭh-kā , One's own; private. gāṅṭh kā paisā , n. m. One's own money. gāṅṭh kā pūrā , A rich, wealthy person. Āṅkh kā andhā, gāṅṭh kā pūrī , Prov. Blind and rich! (A rich fool for a customer). gāṅṭh kā khonā , v. n. 1. To waste or squander one's money. 2. To incur loss; do oneself an injury. gāṅṭh kaṭnā , v. n. 1. To have one's pocket picked. 2. To be robbed, plundered. gāṅṭh yā girah kholnā ; Sant. gheṅṭ raṛāë , v. a. 1. To untie a knot; disentangle; loosen. 2. To open one's purse; to spend. 3. To remove ill-feeling, or misunderstanding. Dil kī gāṅṭh khol ḍālo! gāṅṭh k. , v. a. 1. To put in one's pocket. 2. To hoard up. 3. To misappropriate. gāṅṭh gaṅṭhīlā , adj. 1. Knotted (a stick). Tūṭe se phir-ke juṛe to gāṅṭh gaṭhīlā ho . Prov. Join the broken, a knot's the token. gāṅṭh meṅ rakhnā , v. n. To fill one's pocket or purse; to pocket. ḍeṛh gāṅṭh ; Sant. or asit , A slip knot. gur gāṅṭh , A hard knot.

u: گانتھ کا پورا عقل کا اندھا
def: penny wise and pound foolish
s.m. A knot, &c. ( = gāṅṭh); — the knotted parts of the stalk and the ear-ends of straw separately piled on the threshing-floor (the 'colder' of English agriculture); grain mixed with straw.

s.m. = gaṭṭhā, q.v. s.m. A large bundle, a pack, package, bale; a load; — the wrist, wrist-bone; ankle-joint; pastern (of a horse; see gaṭṭā); — a root (of onion, or of turmeric, &c.), a clove (of garlic); — a knot or division (in a measuring line or chain); the twentieth part of a jarīb (each gaṭṭhā containing three ilāhī gaz, or ninety-nine inches.
n. m. The knotted parts of the stalk and the ear-ends of straw separately piled on the threshing floor; the colder of English agriculture; grain mixed with straw.
n. m. A flake; clove. Piyāz kā ganṭhā .
adj. (f. -ī) = gaṭhīlā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Knotted, knotty; — compact, well-set, robust, sturdy, stout, strong; — a robust man, &c.
s.m. A bricklayer; a builder.
s.m. An iron style (for writing on palm-leaves).
s.f. (dialec.) A knot, &c.
subs: s.m. An occupant of lands by heritable tenure.

def: s.f. A root or flake (of onion, &c.); a clove (see gaṭṭhā).
s.f. = gaṭhiyā, and gāṅṭh, qq.v. s.f. A bundle, package, &c. ( = gaṭṭhī); a bag, sack; a pannier; — a bump (see gaṭṭā); — pain in the joints; rheumatism; gout (in the joints)
subs: or gaṭhiyā-bāʼo, s.f. Rheumatism; gout in the joints.

def: Knot-joining. گنٿهجوڙا باندهنا ganṭh-joṛā bāndhnā, To tie together the skirts of the mantles of the bride and bridegroom: a ceremony attending marriage performed by the purohit or officiating priest.
s. m. A cutpurse.
v.t. To fasten or tie together, to thread, string; to arrange, connect in a regular series; to lay bricks (in mortar).
more: v. n. S. ग्रन्यन 1. To string together; thread. 2. To lay bricks in mortar.
v. a. To lay bricks in mortar, to string, to put or tie in order on a string, &c.

def: v.t. To fasten, tie, knot, string together, thread; to join, make adhere, put together, cobble, mend; to arrange, connect in a regular series; to fasten beads (or other ornaments) separately and at certain distances to the string that passes through and connects them all; — to string words together, to compose; to construct; to concoct; — to interlock; to clasp; — to impede, obstruct; — to win over, attach (to oneself); to reduce to obedience or subjection
more: v. a. 1. To tie, to knot, join, make adhere, stitch, cobble, put together. 2. To reduce to obedience or subjection, to attach.

def: v. a. 1. To thread; string. Hāth meṅ rishta o sozan aur ek kāgaz kā ḍher, Jūtiyān sī gāṅṭhte haiṅ ahl-kār-i-band o bast . Office clerks. Jest. Phabtī . Needle and thread in hand, a pile of papers' high, These settlement officials just like cobblers ply. 2. To fasten; knot; stitch together. 3. To cobble; mend. Jūtā gāṅṭhnā . 4. To interlock; clasp. 5. To concoct; construct; compose. Mazmūn gāṅṭhnā . 6. ( gāṅṭh lenā ) To win or draw over; bring. over to one's side. Dushman ko gāṅṭh līyā .
A dialec. form of gaṭhnā, and gāṅṭhnā, qq.v.

ety: Prk. गंठडओ; S. ग्रन्थ+र+कः; see gaṭhṛī
def: s.m. (Dakh.) A capital stock, fund; bank (for money).
more: s. (dakh.) A capital stock, fund, bank (for money: Harris ).

def: s.f. = gaṭhṛī, q.v. s.f. A bundle, parcel, packet, package, bale; a bag (of money, &c., as that brought in payment of revenue); — amount, total; — property, substance, wealth; — (fig.) a person lying senseless (bundled up, as it were) under drunkenness, &c.; — a crew, pack, junto
subs: To tie up (one's) bundle or baggage, get (one's) things together, to pack up, &c. ( = gaṭhṛī karnā)
(-kī), To make a bundle (of); to get together; to tie up (one's arms and legs together); to double up (an adversary, in wrestling); to sum up, find the total (of), to add
(-kī), To break open the bundle (of), to rob or plunder the bundle (of); to rob.

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Jāṭs.
v.t. = H گانٿ गांट gāṅṭ, s.f. dialec. forms of gāṅṭh, and gāṅṭhnā respectively, qq.v.
s.m. A village, a hamlet
more: n. m. S. ग्राम Pālī, gām'ako . 1. A village. 1. Gāṅv ke gaṅvele, muṅh men khāk, peṭ meṅ ḍhele . Prov. (Said by townspeople). A village boor, dust-soiled, his stomach filled with clods (coarse meal). 2. Pānī meṅ hai vākā gāoṅ, bastī meṅ nahīṅ vākā ṭhāoṅ, le dīyā maiṅ vākā nāoṅ . Riddle. Ans. Nāo a boat. A village on the water see, a settlement it's not, there now you have its name to boot. 2. A foreign country; another province. Gāoṅ gayā, sūṭā jāge . Prov. Who's from the village gone who knows when he 'll come back? Who knows when from his sleep the sleeper will awake? gāṅo asthān , n. m. The site of a village, whether in ruins or still standing. gāṅo bāṅṭ , n. f. Division of villages. gāṅo-jan , n. m. The village community. gāṅo kharch . Municipal or village charges or expenses. gāṅo kā uṭhāo, gāṅo kharch, malbā, gauṅṭā , The municipal expenses of a village; village charges. gāṅo kā bir…
subs: s.m. Site of a village (whether in ruins or still standing)
or gāṅʼo-baṭ, s.f. Division of villages; division of a taʻalluqa into separate villages
or gāṅʼo-ḵẖarć, s.m. Village expenses or charges; municipal expenses
s.f. The cultivation, or the population, of a village
s.m. (f. -inī) = gāṅʼo-wālā, s.m. (f. -ī), A villager, a rustic.

def: s.m. contrac. of gāṅw, q.v.
= H گنوائي गंवाई gaṅwāʼī, = H گنوائين गंवाईं gaṅwāʼīṅ, s.f. corr. of gawāhī, q.v.
= H گنواهي गंवाही gaṅwāhī, = H گنوائين गंवाईं gaṅwāʼīṅ, s.f. corr. of gawāhī, q.v.
= H گنواهي गंवाही gaṅwāhī, = H گنوائي गंवाई gaṅwāʼī, s.f. corr. of gawāhī, q.v.

ety: gaṅwā˚ = Prk. गमावे = S. गम+आपि, caus. aug.
def: v.t. To lose; to lose or miss (a road, or one's way); to throw away, to get rid, of; to spend in vain; to waste, squander; to pass or spend (time); to trifle or fritter away (time, &c.)
more: v. a. S. गम् to go. To waste or squander (money, time). Samman! sāṅjh aṅdher māṅ mūl bāṭ mat chāl! Jān gaṅvāve ek din, sang gaṅvāve māl . Samman. Samman! upon the road at dusk ne'er take your way! Certes you'll, lose your life and eke your wealth one day.
v. a. To lose, to throw away, to get rid of, to discharge, to spend in vain, to waste, to squander, to pass, to trifle.

def: v. a. (dakh.) See گنوانا gaṅwānā.

ety: gāṅw, q.v.+ār = gen. suff. kar, q.v.
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A villager, countryman, peasant, rustic; a clown, boor, churl; a low-bred person; — a stupid fellow, a dolt; — adj. Boorish, clownish; stupid ( = gaṅwārī)
more: n. m. gaṅvārī , n. f. H. gāṅo village, S. ग्रामनर 1. A villager; countryman; peasant; rustic. 1. Gaṅvār gauṅ kā yār . Prov. The village boor his interest knows. 2. Gaṅvār kā hāṅsā toṛe pāṅsā . Prov. The laugh of a clown will break a bone. 2. A clown; boor; churl. 3. A stupid fellow. gaṅvār-pan , n. m. 1. Rudeness. 2. Stupidity. gaṅvār kā laṭh , n. m. lit . A villager's club. A fool; gawk; lout. gaṅvār'ū , adj. Rude; rough. gaṅvārī bolī , n. f. The rustic language. gaṅvar-dal . A company or crowd of rustics.
s. m. A countryman, villager, boor, clown, churl, rustick, peasant.
subs: s.m. A villager's club; — (fig.) a blockhead, dolt, lout, fool.

def: s.m. A company or crowd of rustics.
adj. (dialec.) = gaṅwārī, q.v. adj. Of or belonging to villagers or rustics; rustic, provincial; — clownish; stupid
subs: or gaṅwārī bolī, s.f. Rustic language or speech; provincial brogue.

ety: gaṅwār, q.v.+ī = S. इकः, or ईयः
def: adj. Of or belonging to villagers or rustics; rustic, provincial; — clownish; stupid
subs: or gaṅwārī bolī, s.f. Rustic language or speech; provincial brogue.

ety: gaṅwār, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Rusticity; rudeness; clownishness; stupidity.
adj. & s.m. Rustic; rude, rough; clownish, boorish; — a rustic, &c. ( = gaṅwār).
s.m.f. Prodigal, wasteful; — a waster, squanderer, spendthrift.
adj. & s.m. = gaṅwī.

ety: gāṅw, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to a village or to the country, rural, rustic; — a rustic
more: adj. Relating to a village or the country, rustick.
subs: s.m. pl. Rustic hamlets or villages.

ety: prob. S. ग्रामिन्, = H گنويلا गंवेला gaṅwelā (f. -ī) [Prk. गामइल्लओ; S. ग्राम+इल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. = gaṅwī.
more: adj. Relating to a village or the country, rustick.

def: part. adj. To be counted, admitting of calculation or computation, calculable; requiring counting.
village, hamlet; hometown
ex: پنجاب کے ضلع گورداسپور میں ایک گاؤں نارتھ ویسٹرن ریلوے کے سٹیشن بٹالہ سے بفاصلہ ۱۰۔ میل واقع ہے۔
A village in the Gurdaspur district of Punjab which lies 10 miles away from the North Western railway station of Batala.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi گانوں (gā̃õ) / گاؤں (gāõ) / گانو (gā̃v), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀕𑀸𑀫 (gāma), from Sanskrit ग्राम (grā́ma), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *grā́mas, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *grā́mas, from Proto-Indo-European *(h₂)gróm-os, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂ger- (“to assemble, gather together”).

ety: Pehl. go; Zend gāo; S. गो; Prk. गउओ, गउआ, गाओ
def: s.m. A bull, an ox, a bullock; — s.f. A cow
more: n. f. H. gāë , Eng. cow; S. गाहटा A cow; a bull. Mā rā che az īṅ qissa ki gāo āmadokhar raft . P. Prov. What's it to me that a cow hath come and a donkey's gone. gāo-palaṅg , n. m. A cameleopard; giraffe. gāo-charāī , n. f. 1. Grazing. 2. Grazing or pasture ground. 3. A tax levied on pasture land; a charge for grazing. gāo khurd h. , v. n. To be ruined, destroyed. Woh daftar hī gāo khurd ho gayā . Prov. Saying. gāo-dīdah , n. f. lit . ox-eyed. Bread made in this shape. gāo-zabāṅ . 1. A kind of bread of the shape of an ox-tongue. 2. Bugloss (?); Cacalia kleinia , Wat. gāv-i-zamīn . The bull on whose horns the earth is said to rest. gāo-zorī , n. f. Violence. gāo-shumārī , 1. An enumeration of cattle. 2. A tax upon cattle. gāv-i-ambar . The sea cow, from which the Persians suppose ambergris to come. gāo-khānah , n. m. 1. A cow pen or shed. 2. The place where dead cattle are flayed. gāo-mes…

s. f. A cow. (Sans. गौः ). گاوپلنگ gāw-palang, A camelopard. گاودوش gāw-dosh, A milking-pail, a churn. گاوديده gāw-dīda, A kind of bread. گاوزبان gāw-zabān, 1. Name of a kind of bread. 2. Bugloss. گاوزمين gāw-i-zamīn, The bull on whose horns the earth is fabled to rest. گاو زوري gāw-zor-ī, s. f. Violence. گاوميش gāw-mesh, A buffalo.
subs: s.m. A peculiar sleight by which an antagonist is thrown in wrestling (as an ox is thrown down by a butcher; cf. qasaiyā-dāv); — gāʼo-palang, s.m. A camelopard, a giraffe
s.m. A large pillow or bolster which supports the back of a person sitting
s.f. Grazing cattle; pasture-ground; — charge for grazing; — a tax levied on pasture-land
s.m. House or stall for cattle; cow-pen; cow-shed; — place where dead cattle are flayed
adj. Broken to pieces, ruined, destroyed
adj. Thick at one end and thin at the other; tapering, sloping; — s.f. A cow-tail; anything tapering; a cone; a pyramid; — a trumpet; a tube; — acclivity, descent
s.m. lit. 'Ox-eye'; a kind of bread of the shape of an ox-eye
s.f. A kind of bread of a long shape resembling neats' tongues; — the plant ox-tongue, bugloss
s.m. The bull on whose horns the earth is fabled to rest
or gāʼo-sar, s.m. The club or mace of Farīdūn
s.f. Enumeration (or census) of cattle; — a tax upon cattle
s.f. The (medicinal) gum of the opoponax tree; gum-resin
s.f. The sea-cow (from which the Persians suppose ambergris to come)
s.f. Killing a cow or an ox; slaughtering cows
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَاو (gāw). Doublet of گائے (gāe).

ety: gāʼo, q.v.+dī = Zend di = S. धी
def: adj. & s.m. 'Ox-witted,' stupid, ignorant, foolish; — a blockhead, booby, simpleton, fool.
adj. 1. Tapering. 2. Conical or tiprooted. gāo-dum , n. f. A cone.
more: 1. adj. Thick at one end and thin at the other, taper. 2. Sloping, acclivity, descent. 3. s. f. A trumpet, a tube. 4. A cone (Arab. مخروط), a pyramid.

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. S. ग्रभ्
def: s.m. Flattery, wheedling; deceit; artifice; fraud; embezzlement; — one who embezzles ( = next; — cf. ghāʼū-ghap)
more: s. m. f. (dakh.) Deceit, artifice: see فريب.

def: s.m.f. Flattering; alluring; deceitful, fraudulent; — a flatterer, deceiver, humbug; — one who misappropriates or embezzles.
adj. Deceitful, alluring.
s.m. A cowherd.
s.m. = کانو gāṅw or gāṅʼo, q.v. s.m. A village, a hamlet
subs: s.m. Site of a village (whether in ruins or still standing)
or gāṅʼo-baṭ, s.f. Division of villages; division of a taʻalluqa into separate villages
or gāṅʼo-ḵẖarć, s.m. Village expenses or charges; municipal expenses
s.f. The cultivation, or the population, of a village
s.m. (f. -inī) = gāṅʼo-wālā, s.m. (f. -ī), A villager, a rustic.

def: v.t. & n. (dialec.) = gānā, q.v. s.f. Freedom from need or want; competence, sufficiency; richness, wealthiness; riches, wealth; — content.
s.m. Expense incurred in the municipal administration of a village; village charges (syn. gāṅʼo-ḵẖarć).
adj. Of or belonging to a village.

n. f. Gum resin; opopanax (?); a medicinal gum. gāo-kushī , n. f. Killing kine; slaughter of cows. gāo-ghap, gāū-ghap, ghāū ghap ; Bhoj. ghauā ghap , n. m. Embezzlement (غبن). gāo ghap , n. m. One who embezzles. gāo ghap k. , v. a. To embezzle ( gaban k .).
more: s. f. Name of a medicinal gum or gum resin, opoponax.

ety: prob. akin to P. guft, q.v.; cf. S. जल्पः
def: s.f. Prattle, talk, chat, tattle, gossip, idle talk, yarn, false report
more: n. f. 1. Tattle; gossip ( bak ). 2. A false report; a shave ( afwāh ). gap gap yā gapā gap khānā , v. a. To gulp down. gapeṅ mārnā, yā hāṅknā , v. a. To gossip; tittle tattle.
s. f. Prattle, tattle, talk, chat. گپ مارنا gap mārnā, v. a. To prattle, to tattle.
subs: (or gapeṅ) mārnā (-kī), To prate (about), to tattle, to gossip, &c.
wety: Either onomatopoeic and inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑀧𑁆𑀧- (*gappa-), or inherited from Sanskrit गल्प (galpa). Cognate with Classical Persian گَپ (gap), Punjabi ਗੱਪ (gappa) / گپّ (gappa), Bengali গপ (gop), Gujarati ગપ (gap), Nepali गफ (gaph), Odia ଗପ (gapa), Assamese গপ (gop).

def: s.m. (local), The sound made by a blow, or in swallowing, &c.; — bragging, boasting, boast
more: s. m. (dakh.) The sound or smack of a blow. غپ دني g̠ẖap dane (dakh.) All at once, in a crack.
s. m. (dakh.) Brag, boast. غپ مارنا g̠ẖap mārnā, To brag, to boast, to crack, to swagger ( Harris ).

def: s.m. & adv. The sound made in swallowing or gulping down anything; — gulp-gulp; with a gulping sound, or with repeated gulps; — abundantly, copiously
more: Slang, n. m. 1. The sound made in gulping down anything. 2. Vox congressus in coitu. gapā-gap , adv. Abundantly; in showers. Gapā-gap nūr baras rahā hai . (Fac.)
subs: v.t. To swallow with gulps, to gulp down.

def: v.t. To swallow with a noise, to gulp, to bolt; — to catch (as a ball, &c.).
more: v. a. 1. To swallow; gulp. 2. To catch (a ball).
v. a. To swallow, to gulp.

def: Nonsense.
n. f. Confusion; mess.
s.f. (colloq.) = gap-shap, q.v.s.v. gap. see گپ شپ gap-shap.
s.m. & adv. = gapagap, q.v. (cf. gab-gab). s.m. & adv. The sound made in swallowing or gulping down anything; — gulp-gulp; with a gulping sound, or with repeated gulps; — abundantly, copiously
subs: v.t. To swallow with gulps, to gulp down.

def: adj. & s.m. Prating, tattling, addicted to gossip or to yarning, &c.; — prater, idle talker, tattler, babbler, gossip, yarner.
s.m. (local), The sound made by a blow, or in swallowing, &c.; — bragging, boasting, boast
more: s. m. (dakh.) The sound or smack of a blow. غپ دني g̠ẖap dane (dakh.) All at once, in a crack.

adj. Plumpness; roundness. Uṭhtī javānī, baiṭhtā gappā . Prov. Youth upspringing, rounded charms firm setting. gappā khānā, Slang , v. n. To have carnal intercourse with. gap-chavval , adj. Plump; fat; round.
adj. & s.m. Prating, tattling, addicted to gossip or to yarning, &c.; — prater, idle talker, tattler, babbler, gossip, yarner.
more: s. A tattler, an idle talker.

def: s.m. (local), Boaster, bragger, swaggerer.
more: adj. (dakh.) Boaster, cracker, swaggerer, bragger ( Harris ).

def: s.m. Hard soil of a whitish appearance.
s. f. Prattle, tattle.
wety: Reduplication of گپ (gap, “gossip”).

def: see گپ شپ gap-shap.
more: s. (dakh.) Confabulation, talkativeness, garrulity, prate, gossip ( Harris ): see گپ شپ gap-shap.

ety: v.n. fr. غور 'to go down, sink,' &c.
def: s.m. A cave, cavern, pit, hole, den or lair (of a wild beast); a grotto; a hollow, low or depressed land or ground (where water lies); — a deep gash or wound
more: n. m. 1. A cavern ( bhaṭ , 2). 2. A hollow or low ground where water lies. 3. A deep gash or wound. gār paṛnā, yā paṛ jānā , v. n. To become hollow. 2. To become a deep sore or ulcer.
s. m. A cavern, a pit. يار غار yār-i-g̠ẖār, A companion in a cave, (meaning) an intimate friend. (See under يار yār). ¶ g̠ẖārr (part. act. of غرّ) s. m. A deceiver.
subs: (-meṅ), A hollow to form (in), to become hollow; a deep wound or sore to form.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَار (ġār), borrowed from Arabic غَار (ḡār). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi غار (ġar /⁠ġār⁠/).

def: s.f. = gārī, or gālī, q.v. adj. High, excessive, exorbitant; high-priced, dear; precious.
more: ( In Compos .) Doer, agent, as parhez-gār, khidmat-gār .

def: s.f. (Dakh.) Hail; — frost; — an agate.
A suffix denoting 'Maker, doer, agent' (e.g. ḵẖidmat-gār; parhez-gār; gunah-gār, &c., q.v.).
s.m. A drink, beverage, fluid; — a factitious poison; poison; — an antidote; — sickness, disease
more: ( In compos .) A maker; doer; workman; as, qalaī-gar .
1. (in compos.) A maker, worker, workman. 2. (contracted from اگر) If. 3. s. f. (dakh.) Scab, mange (as, of a horse).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = gal, q.v. part. adj. Rolling, wallowing (used as last member of compounds).
s.f. Itch in the throat and breast (of a horse); farcy.

ety: Zend kara; S. कर; cf. gār
def: s.m. Doer; maker; worker, workman (used as a suffix, e.g. zar-gar, 'a worker in gold, a goldsmith').
s.m. (dialec.) = gaṛh, q.v. s.f. A difficulty; a misfortune.
more: s. m. For گڙهہ gaṛh, q. v.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi गुड (guḍa), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀼𑀟 (guḍa), from Sanskrit गुड (guḍá). Cognate to Punjabi ਗੁੜ (guṛ), Gujarati ગોળ (goḷ), Marathi गूळ (gūḷ), Bengali গুড় (guṛ).

def: conj. If; — gar-ći, conj. Although.

ety: for A. غارة, n. of un. fr. غار
def: s.m. A cave, &c. ( = g̠ār, q.v.).
more: s. m. Cave: see غار g̠ẖār.

def: s.m. Hollow, hole, cavity, cavern; a ditch; low land (on which water does not lie long); — ambuscade
subs: To be concealed, to lie in ambush.

def: s.m. = گاڙا gāṛā, q.v.
s.m. = galā, q.v. s.m. A pod of cotton; a flock of cotton; a ball of carded cotton.
more: s. m. Throat, neck. See گلا galā.

def: s.m. = garāʼo, q.v. s.m. A stock or collection of sheaves; — a chopper (to cut fodder with; — syn. gaṅḍāsā).
s.m. A large sheaf; a bundle of sheaves ( = garā, and garāʼo)
more: s. m. Latch (of a door: Harris ).
subs: s.f. Division of produce (or a crop) without threshing, by stacking the sheaves in proportionate shares.

def: s.m. Kneaded or prepared mud (see gārā); — a sort of coarse, thick cloth (see gāṛhā); — a sect of Moḥammadans forcibly converted from Hindūism in the reign of 'Alamgīr.
s.m. Thick mud; mud kneaded or prepared for making bricks, or pottery, or for building or plastering; — name of a rāginī or musical mode
more: n. m. 1. Mud; kneaded clay. 2. A musical mode. gārā k. , v. a. To knead clay. gārā gānā , v. a. To tell a long story. Miyāṅ! tum apnā hī gārā gā rahe ho!

n. m. S. गर्त्त a hollow. 1. Low land on which water does not lie long. (?) 2. A sect of Mahomedans forcibly converted from Hinduism in the reign of Ālamgīr. Gāṛā aur jāṛā chhete binā nāhīṅ māne . Rus. Gāṛā and grass must be beaten.
s. m. 1. Mud (kneaded or) prepared for making pottery, prepared clay. 2. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.
subs: To prepare mud for plastering or building, &c.
To sing a gārā; (fig.) to tell a long story.

def: s.m. A common load-cart, a cart without a frame or box; a cart, waggon; gun-carriage.
more: s. m. A ditch, pit, cavern, an ambuscade. گاڙي بيٿهنا gāṛe baiṭhnā, To be concealed, to lie in ambush.
s. m. A cart. See گاڙي gāṛī.

def: n. f. A division of a crop by stacking the sheaves in shares.

ety: prob. gaṛ = gaṭ or ghaṭ = S. घटा+S. गच्छः, or गुच्छः
def: s.m. (dialec.) Thick close wood (or branches); a thicket; — denseness; crowdedness; — adv. Closely, densely; in a crowded or pressed manner.
more: s. (dakh.) Thicket or thick close wood or branches (?): see غڙچه g̠ẖaṛachcha.

def: s.m. Guard; watch, ward; — a guard-room.
more: n. m. Cor. of Guard. 1. Watch; ward. 2. A guard room.

def: s. (dakh.) Thicket or thick close wood or branches (?): see غڙچه g̠ẖaṛachcha.
adj. & s.m. Strange, wonderful; — strangers; — s.f. Strange or wonderful things or events.
s.f. pl. (of g̠arāʼib), Strange or wonderful things.
s.f. (rustic or dialec.) = garaj, q.v.
v.n. To thunder; to roar; to bellow; to crack with a report (as pearls, &c.)
more: v. n. S. गर्ज्जन Pālī, gajjanam . 1. To thunder; roar; bellow. ' E sakhī, voh garjat hai.' 'Kyā, sakhī, vah bādar hai?' 'E sakhī, voh chamkat hai.' 'Kyā, sakhī, voh bijlī hai?' 'E sakhī, voh hāthoṅ sohe.' 'Kyā, sakhī, voh kaṅgan hai?' 'E sakhī, voh mukh pe sohe.' 'Kyā, sakhī, voh besar hai? ' Riddle. Ans. Huqqā a pipe. 'See, friend, it gives a rumble loud,' 'Then it's a cloud.' 'See, how it brightens all around!' 'It's lightning, I'll be bound.' 'Upon the hand a grace is set,' 'Is it a bracelet?' 'The face too it does beauty lend,' 'A nose-ring, friend?' 2. To crack. Motī garaj gayā. garaj'vā , n. m. A vegetable supposed to grow under the influence of thunder.
v. n. To thunder, to roar, bellow. ¶ gurajna, To snarl.
wety: Adapted from Sanskrit गर्जति (gárjati). Doublet of گَاجْنَا (gājnā). Compare Maithili गरजब (garᵊjab) / 𑒑𑒩𑒖𑒥 (garajaba), Punjabi ਗਰਜਣਾ (garjaṇā) / گَرجݨا (garjṇā), Gujarati ગરજવું (garajvũ), Marathi गरजणे (garajṇe).

ety: garaj(nā) + wā = iwā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. A kind of vegetable (supposed to grow under the influence of thunder).
s.m. A kind of gold-fish, or gilt-head.
s.m. Poison, venom; venom of a snake.
more: n. m. Poison ( bis ); venom. Jib Bhagvān rog ne ṭhāve sāṅp garal aukhad ho jāve . Rus. When God is pleased disease to heal, snake's venom makes a wholesome meal.

def: adj. & s.m. Poisonous, venomous; — a venomous animai.

ety: for A. غرامة, inf. n. of غرم 'to pay, discharge,' &c.
def: s.f. A debt; a fine, mulct; tribute.

adj. Z. gerāṅ . See بهاري 1, 11, 12, and اجيرن 7, 10, 11. 1. Grievous ( ajīran , 11). 2. Dear ( akrā ). garāṅ-bahā , adj. 1. High-priced; costly. 2. Precious. garāṅ-khātir , adj. Heavy; burdensome; unpleasant. garāṅ k. , v. a. To raise; enhance (the price). garāṅ guzarnā, yā mālūm h. , v. n. To lie heavy upon one; to be unpleasant. garāṅ h. , v. n. 1. To be grievous; burdensome. 2. To rise (in price).
s.f. 'A place into which one sinks,' a swamp, morass.

more: (caus. of گڙنا) v. a. To cause to penetrate, &c.
