n. m. An enclosure of mud round a mill. Garanḍ chūn bin sagrā rītā, Bahū kahe 'pīsat din bītā! ' Rus. About the mill no flour at all I find, The daughter-in-law says 'all day long I grind!'
s.f. Boasting, boast, vaunt, brag (syn. baṛang)
subs: or gaṛang hāṅknā, To boast, brag.

def: n. f. Brag; boast. gaṛaṅg hāṅknā, yā mārnā , v. n. To brag; boast. gaṛaṅg' yā, garaṅgī , n. m. A boaster; braggart.
s.m. A boaster, a braggart.
s.m. A boaster, a braggart.

n. f. An effigy, or the name of a person which is buried in the ground after certain incantations. This ceremony is performed for the destruction of any person ( māran ), to secure his affection ( mohan ), to subject him to obedience ( basikaran ), to imprison him, or deprive him of the power of action or speech ( stambhan ), to drive him away ( uchchāṭan ), or to bring him before one ( ākarshan ).

ety: prob. Prk. गड्डंतं, imperf. part. of गड्डणअं = gaṛnā, q.v.
def: s.m. The game of tip-cat; — a ceremony performed to remove the influence of an evil spirit (an effigy of the demon is made of dough and painted with red lead (sendūr), and to this offerings are made; it is then shown to the sick person or passed over him from head to foot, after which the effigy is burnt and the charm is completed); — a mode of incantation for the destruction of any person (māraṇ), or to secure his affection (mohan), or to subject him to obedience (basīkaran), or to imprison him or deprive him of the power of action or speech (stambhan), or to drive him away (uććāṭan), or to call him (ākarshaṇ; — certain words are pronounced over an effigy of the person (or his name in writing) which is after wards buried).

ety: prob. Prk. गड्डंतं, imperf. part. of गड्डणअं = gaṛnā, q.v.
def: s.m. The game of tip-cat; — a ceremony performed to remove the influence of an evil spirit (an effigy of the demon is made of dough and painted with red lead (sendūr), and to this offerings are made; it is then shown to the sick person or passed over him from head to foot, after which the effigy is burnt and the charm is completed); — a mode of incantation for the destruction of any person (māraṇ), or to secure his affection (mohan), or to subject him to obedience (basīkaran), or to imprison him or deprive him of the power of action or speech (stambhan), or to drive him away (uććāṭan), or to call him (ākarshaṇ; — certain words are pronounced over an effigy of the person (or his name in writing) which is after wards buried).
more: s. m. 1. Name of a game, tipcat. 2. A mode of incantation performed for the destruction of any person ( māraṇ ), to secure his affection ( mohan ), to subject him to obedience ( basīkaraṇ ), to imprison him or deprive him of the power of action or speech ( stambhan ), to drive him away ( uchchāṭan ), or to call him ( ākarsh̤an ). Certain words are pronounced over an effigy of the person, or his name in writing, which is afterwards buried.

def: s.m. Neck-rope (for securing an animal); a tether.
s.m. A coarse kind of salt.
s.m. A burying.
s.m. A stock or collection of sheaves; — a chopper (to cut fodder with; — syn. gaṅḍāsā).
more: n. m. 1. A bundle of sheaves. 2. A chopper for cutting fodder ( gaṅḍāsā ).

def: s.m. An instrument for cutting jawāṛ stalks, &c. for fodder; a kind of chopper ( = garā, or garāʼo).

ety: prob. fr. S. गर, rt. गॄ+gap; see gap-gap, and gapaknā
def: adj. Taken down, swallowed up, lost; — s.m. A gulp
subs: or gaṛap-denī, adv. In the giving of a gulp, in a trice, suddenly; — down you go, in you go, down with you, &c. (e.g. gaṛap thailī-meṅ, 'down with you (or down you go) into the bag').

def: n. m. A gulp. gaṛap, gaṛap-denī , adv. In a crack; suddenly. Chūṅ chūṅ, gaṛap thailī meṅ! Pipe, pipe! down into the bag! (The fowler to the captive bird)
adj. corr. of g̠arq, q.v.

s.m. (dialec.) = garuḍ, q.v. s.m. (in Myth.) Name of a mystical bird, the chief of the feathered race, the enemy of the serpent race, and the vehicle of Vishṇu; — a large kind of crane, the adjutant-bird, Ardea argala (syn. haṛ-gillā); — a large vulture; — the great horn-bill, Homraius bicornis; — name of a peculiar military array
subs: s.m. Name of the seventeenth Purāṇa (it is the one commonly read after the death of a Hindū); (fig.) an old story.

ety: prob. corr. of A. غرغرة; or A. غرارة, inf. n. of غرّ
def: s.m. Gargling; a gargle.
s.m. A sack for holding the walls (qanāt) of a tent; (loc. or dialec.) an enclosing or encircling wall or fence; a line of investment, &c.
more: n. m. A sack for holding the qanāts or walls of a tent.
s. m. A sack (for holding the walls of a tent, &c.).

ety: prob. S. ग्रह+कारिका; cf. ghirnī or ghernī
def: s.f. An instrument for twisting thread or string, a reel; the pulley or block over which a well-rope passes; (loc. or dialec.) a groove, a rut; the groove or rut in which moves the wheel of a mill for preparing mortar, &c.
more: n. f. 1. A reel for twisting thread, or string. 2. The block over which the well-rope passes.
s. f. An instrument for twisting thread or string.

def: s.m. = gaḍariyā, gaṛeriyā, qq.v. s.m. A shepherd (by caste or occupation), a goat-herd; a cowherd
more: s. m. A shepherd or goatherd. See گڐريا gaḍariyā.
subs: s.m. A shepherd's tale; — rustic lore
A song of shepherds; a pastoral.

def: s.m. A snake-charmer, a juggler, conjurer.
s.m. = gāraṛu, q.v. s.m. A snake-charmer, a juggler, conjurer.
s.m. = gāraṛu, q.v. s.m. A snake-charmer, a juggler, conjurer.
s.f. = gaṇḍāsī, q.v. s.f. A small chopper for cutting sugar-cane, &c. (see ganḍāsā).
s.f. = gaṇḍāsī, q.v. s.f. A small chopper for cutting sugar-cane, &c. (see ganḍāsā).

ety: for A. غارة, fr. اغارة ig̠ārat, 'making a raid,' &c., inf. n. of اغار, iv of غور 'to come to the low land or country,' &c.
def: s.f. (orig.) 'A raid, foray'; plunder, pillage, rapine, havoc, devastation
more: n. f. Pillage; rapine (اجارَ). gārat-gol , n. m. Sudden loss or destruction. gārat-gol h. , v. n. 1. To be ruined; destroyed. 2. To be annihilated. 3. To disappear. gārat k. , v. a. To spoil; despoil; lay waste (اجارَنا 3-7). Khudā tujhe gārat kare! gārat-gar , n. m. A plunderer ( baṭmār ). gārat-gayā, gārtī , Wom. Ruined; destroyed. 1. Chhoṛ, gārat-gaë! merā pīchhā! Wom. Shauq. Away! thou blasted one! pursue me not! 2. Mue gārti māntā nahin . Mah. Wom. gārat h. , v. n. To be destroyed, ruined, etc. Pahuṅchī woh kahīṅ zamīn pe jā-ke, Gārat huī dam meṅ luṭ luṭā-ke . Sohan Lāl.

s. (when compounded with a verb, this latter may be mas. or fem. agreeable to the gender of the object of the action) Rapine, plunder, devastation. غارت غول g̠ẖārat-g̠ẖol, 1. s. m. Sudden loss or destruction. 2. adj. Suddenly or unexpectedly lost. غارت کرنا g̠ẖārat karnā, To plunder, ravage, spoil. غارتگر g̠ẖārat-gar, A plunderer, an oppressor.
subs: v.n. To be plundered, be spoiled, be marred, be destroyed or ruined

s.m. Sudden loss or destruction; — adj. Suddenly or unexpectedly lost; — g̠ārat-g̠ol honā or ho-jānā, To be suddenly or unexpectedly lost; to be swept away, to be ruined or destroyed, to be annihilated; to disappear
v.t. To plunder, spoil, ravage, lay waste, ruin, destroy
s.f. The act of plundering, or of laying waste, &c.; pillage; destruction

def: s.m. Light soil, poor soil.

ety: inf. n. of غرض 'to be vexed or disquieted' (by
def: s.f. An object of aim or pursuit, or of desire, or of want; aim, end, object, design, view, purpose, intention; business; meaning; a want, need, necessity, occasion; interest, concern; interestedness, interested motive; spite, hatred, rancour; — adv. In short, in a word, in fine
more: n. f. غرض aim. See (اراده 1—4, 6). 1. Interest; concern. Garaz bāolī hotī hai . Prov. Interest is a mad driver. 2. Necessity; occasion ( ehtiyāt ). Tumhārī sau dafa garaz paṛe to lo! (Said by a shopkeeper to an unwilling customer). Buy when you feel the need of it a hundred times! 3. Motive; object. garaz, al-garz . In short ( al-qissah ). garaz rakhnā , v. n. To be interested in; to occupy or concern oneself with. garaz-i-ām . A common object. garaz kā bāolā . A slave to one's passions. garaz kā yār, garzī ; Rus. garjī , adj. 1. Self-seeking; selfish; time-serving; mercenary. 2. Designing; artful. garaz k . (Shopkeepers) v. a. To sell or dispose of; sell cheap. garaz-mand, garzī, sāhib-i-garaz , adj. 1. Needy ( hājatmand ). Khudā dard-maṅd kare, garaz-maṅd na kare! Prov. God feeling hearts all make, a selfish heart ne'er make! 2. Interested; concerned. Matlab ke garzī, yār, pīt paihchān…

1. s. f. Design, view, wish, intention, object, aim, purpose, end, business, meaning, need, occasion, use, want, interestedness, interest, selfishness, hatred, spite. 2. adv. In short, in a word, in fine. غرض آشنا g̠ẖaraẓ-āshnā, adj. Interested. غرض آلود g̠ẖaraẓ-ālūd, Designing, interested, selfish. غرض آميز g̠ẖaraẓ-āmez, Designing, selfish, interested, spiteful. غرض باولا g̠ẖaraẓ-bāʼolā, A slave to oneʼs passions. غرض مند g̠ẖaraẓ-mand, adj. Desirous, wishing for, selfish, designing. غرضمندي g̠ẖaraẓ-mand-ī, s. f. Selfishness.
subs: or g̠araẓ-āmez, adj. Designing; interested, selfish; spiteful, malevolent
or g̠araẓ-kā bāʼolā, s.m. A slave to (one's) desires or passions
(-kī), To feel the need (of); to have occasion (for)
(-kī), To have desire (for), to aim (at); to be interested (in); to busy or concern ooneself (with)
adj. Desirous (of), wishing (for); needing, needy; concerned, interested; selfish, designing
(apnī), To attain (one's) object, gain (one's) end, accomplish (one's) desire or purpose
adj. & adv. Disinterested, having no concern (with), having no purpose to serve; — disinterestedly; indifferently
wety: First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi غَرَض (ġrẓ), borrowed from Classical Persian غَرَض (ġaraz), from Arabic غَرَض (ḡaraḍ).

ety: g̠araẓ, q.v. + Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adv. From interested motives, interestedly, selfishly; spitefully, malevolently.
adj. Interested, self-seeking, selfish, having some end to serve, designing, artful.

ety: inf. n. of غرب 'to retire from view,' 'to disappear,' &c.
def: s.m. Setting (of the sun, or a star); — the west (syn. mag̠rib).
more: n. m. غرب was hidden. The west.
s. m. The setting of the sun, the west.

def: s.m. 1˚ = garbh, q.v.; — 2˚ = garv, q.v.

ety: gaṛ˚ = gaḍ = gaṭ = ghaṭ = S. घटा; baṛ is prob. a jingle; cf. gaḍ-baḍ, gaḍ-maḍ, and gaṭ-paṭ
def: s.m. Disorder, derangement, irregular intermixture, jumble, muddle; confusion, commotion, tumult, disturbance; clamour, uproar, hubbub; hurry, bustle, stir, pother; mental disturbance, distraction, bewilderment; rugged or interrupted state of ground, uneven or broken ground; — adj. In confusion, in a muddle, jumbled, mixed up; higgledy-piggledy, topsy-turvy; miscellaneous, motley, promiscuous; — rough, rugged, uneven (ground)
more: adv. See گڐبڐ gaḍbaḍ. گڙبڙ هونا gaṛbaṛ honā (dakh.) To be confused or alarmed.
adj. (dakh.) Miscellaneous, motley ( Harris ). s. Disturbance, confusion (?).
subs: s.m. Foul weather; disorder; disturbance, &c. ( = gaṛbaṛ)
v.t. To confuse, confound, jumble, mix up; to make a mess of, &c.
v.n. To be confused, to be mixed up, to be jumbled; to be in a muddle; to be distracted, or alarmed, &c.

def: n. m. 1. Disorder; confusion. Gaṛ baṛ gaṛ baṛ bol napūtā dinoṅ mujhāṅ huṛ lāve saë, Bāt jūī kī kahai na khullāṅ, ūṅṭ ḍhāl baṛṛāve saë . Rus. This childless wretch keeps muttering all day long, I have no peace, He wont speak out, but camel-like his grumblings never cease. (Said of her husband). 2. Mismanagement; anarchy. gaṛ baṛ jhālā , n. m. 1. Foul weather. 2. Disturbance; disorder. gaṛ baṛ k. , v. a. 1. See gaḍ maḍ k . 2. To confuse; confound.
s.m. Confusion; bustle; agitation, alarm, &c. ( = gaṛbaṛ); — rumbling (of the bowels), borborygmi.
s.f. Confusion, commotion, disturbance, tumult, bustle; flutter, agitation; scare, fright, alarm ( = gaṛbaṛ; cf. ghabrāhaṭ).
more: s. f. Confusion, fright, alarm, bustle.

def: v. n. To be confused.
v.n. To be confused, to be confounded; to be disturbed (in mind); to be alarmed; — to rumble (the bowels; — in this sense also guṛbuṛānā).
more: v. n. (dakh.) To be alarmed or confused.

def: s.f. (Dakh.) = gaṛbaṛāhaṭ.
s.f. = garvatā, q.v. s.f. Pride, &c. ( = garv).
more: s. f. 1. Pride. 2. A proud woman.

def: n. m. S. ग्रह to conceive, Pālī, gabbo . 1. (Sant. asiaṛ hoṛmo ) Pregnancy. Jā din teṅ Harī garbhaīṅ āë, Sakal lok sukh sampati chhāë . Rāmāyan. 2. Foetus; embryo. 3. (Rus. gabar, gumar ) Pride; vanity. 1. Bheṛ karan se ḍartī nāhīṅ, īsī ho gaī mastānī, Dhan par gabar huā saë tan ne, bāt kare saë man-mānī . Rus. To fight too ready all, intoxicated grown, You say just what you please, with pride of wealth outblown. 2. Ūṅcho ūṅcho sab chaleṅ, nīcho chale na koë, Tulsī! nīcho woh chale jo garbh se ūṅcho hoë . All swell and strut, humility in none is seen, Tulsi! he humbly walks whose pride hath humbled been. garabh ādhān , n. m. A ceremony performed on the first indications of pregnancy, one of the sanskāras or essential rites of Hinduism. garbh-pāt, garbh-pātan , Miscarriage; abortion. garbh-pāt k., garabh girānā ; Sant. dasāo giḍi , v. a. To cause or procure abortion. garbh-pāt h., yā girnā , v. n. To miscarry. garabh-ḍhainā, yāgirnā , v. n. To fall (one's pride). garbh rahnā , v. n. To conceive. garabh k. , v. n. To be proud. garabh se h., garbh meṅ paṛnā , v. n. To be pregnant. garabh-vantā, garabh-vantī, garab-vatā , adj. garbh-vantī, garbhanī ; Sant. gorobīā hor , adj. Pregnant; with child.
wety: From Sanskrit गर्भ (garbha).

def: s.m. Womb; belly; inside, middle, interior (of anything); — fœtus, embryo; — conception, pregnancy; — the fruit (of plants); a sprout; — the bed of a river (especially of the Ganges) when fullest, i.e. on the fourteenth day of the dark half of the month Bhādoṅ, or in the height of the rains (as high as the river flows at this period, so far extends the garbh; after which the tīr or proper bank begins and extends for one hundred and fifty cubits; this space being holy ground)
subs: (˚bha+ādh˚), s.m. Impregnation; conception; — a ceremony performed after menstruation to ensure or facilitate conception; — a ceremony performed on the first indications of pregnancy
s.m. The hair of a new-born child
or garbh-pātan, s.m. Miscarriage (after the fourth month of pregnancy), abortion; — garbh-pāt karnā, To cause or produce abortion
adj. Causing miscarriage; — s.m. A red kind of Moringa (supposed to produce abortion)
s.m. 'A slave by birth'; the son of a slave-girl
or garbh-rūpī, s.m. A child, an infant; a youth (cf. gabrū)
(-ko), To become pregnant, to conceive; — garbh-se honā, v.n. To be pregnant
s.m. The abode of the fœtus, the womb
To dwell in the womb (said of an avatār, q.v.)
or garbh-vantī, adj. & s.f. Pregnant, with child; — a pregnant woman

def: abortion
adj. Impregnated (with); pregnant, with child.
adj. & s.f. Pregnant; teeming; — a pregnant woman; — the plant Asclepias rosea.
more: s. f. Pregnant, with child, teeming.

def: adj. Pregnant.
adj. Pregnant.
adj. Western, occidental.
adj. = garvit, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Proud, haughty, arrogant; vain, conceited; — a proud man, &c.; — garvitā, vulg. garvatā, s.f. A proud woman, &c.
A proud woman.
(for اگرچه agarchi ) conj. Although.
conj. Although (see gar).

ety: rt. of gardīdan; Pehl. vart˚; Zend vareṭ; S. वर्त् (वृत्)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Going round, revolving; traversing, travelling or wandering over, or through, or in (used as last member of compounds, e.g. jahān-gard, 'One who has travelled over or around the world); — s.f. Dust; — the globe; — fortune
more: n. f. Dust. gard-ābād , adj. 1. Dusty. 2. See barbād. Aisā ḍuk mārūṅgā ki gard-ābād ho jāēgā . 3. ( Slang ) Unconscious; senseless. Bukhār meṅ gardā bād paṛā hai. gard-ābād k . See barbād k . gard uṛānā , v. a. To raise the dust. gard-uṛāī , n. m. A tax formerly levied on travellers. gard uṛnā , v. n. 1. To fly about (dust). 2. To be desolate (a city). gard-ālūd, gard-ālūdah , adj. Covered with dust; dusty. gard k . 1. To overshadow; dim; eclipse. 2. To dirty (one's clothes). gard h. , v. n. 1. To be worthless, good-for-nothing. Apne ālam meṅ ye āṭā dāl bhī kyā fard hai, Husn kī ān o adā sab uske āge gard hai . Nazīr. 2. To be practicable or easy. Mehnat ke āge sab gard hai .

1. s. f. Dust. گردآلود gard-ālūd or گرد آلوده gard-ālūda, adj. Defiled with dust, dusty. 2. part. act. (from گرديدن) in compos. Going round, traversing, travelling over or through, wandering over or in.
subs: s.f. A tax formerly levied on travellers
(-kī), Dust to fly about; (fig.) to be or become ruined or desolate (a city, &c.)
or gard-ālūda, adj. Covered (or soiled) with dust, dusty; — encumbered with riches or worldly cares
v.t. To make dusty, to dirty (clothes, &c.); to make dim, to dim, overshadow, cast a gloom over; to eclipse
v.n. To be or become dust
to be worthless or good-for-nothing; to be as dust
to be easily removed or overcome, to be practicable or easy (e.g. měḥnat-ke āge sab gard hai).

def: s.f. = gārad, q.v. s.m. Guard; watch, ward; — a guard-room.
s.m. corr. of gard, 'dust,' q.v. s.m. Guard; watch, ward; — a guard-room.
s.m. Dust ( = gard); a kind of powder.
more: n. m. Z. gordah . 1. A kidney; liver. 2. Courage ( bahādurī ). 3. The scraper of a sugar-mill which prevents the sugar from adhering to the bottom of the boiler. gurde-numā , ( Bot .) Kidney-shaped. gurde kī sūjan , n. f. Inflammation of the kidney. baṛe gurde kā ādmī . A brave man.

n. m. 1. See (قرص 1, 4). 2. A shaven crown. 3. A circle; circumference; circuit. 4. A round tray or platter. 5. A round carpet spread under a hukkā . 6. The barrel of a drum. 7. ( geṅduā ) A round pillow. sūraj kā girdah . ( Ast .) The sun's disc.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُرْدَه (gurda).

def: s.m. An ass ( = gadhā); — the white lotus.
s.f. A she-ass ( = gadhī); — a kind of beetle generated (and living) in cow-dung, a dung-beetle.

n. f. See phūaṛ . 1, 2. Maiṅ to kahā thā 'sāg toṛ lā! Dekho, be yāro! jaṛ pāṛe khaṛī! Gaṛdallo lugāī mere palle paṛī! ' Song. 'Some potherbs pluck,' I said to her. 'Just see, my friends, the roots torn up she stands still there! Such a stupid wife I am tied to!'
s.f. The neck
more: n. f. The neck. gardan uṛānā, kāṭnā, yā mārnā ; E. gardan nāpnā , v. a. To behead; decapitate. gardan par bojh h. , v. n. 1. To be under an obligation. gardan par khūn h., yā rahnā , v. n. To be guilty of the sin of shedding blood. gardan par savār h. , v. n. 1. To sit on one's head. 2. To extort; compel. gardan phaṅsānā , v. a. 1. To put one's head in a noose. 2. To incur responsiblity. gardan jhukānā , v. a. 1. To bow one's head. 2. To yield; to submit. gardan-i-zamīn . An isthmus ( khāknāë ). gardan kā mankā ḍhalnā , v. n. lit . to fall on one side (the neck). To be dying. Jahāṅ gardan kā mankā ḍhalā aur ādmī marā. gardān kaṭnā , v. n. 1. To be beheaded. 2. To be ruined, undone. gardan marvānā , v. a. To cause to be beheaded. gardan maroṛnā , v. a. 1. To throttle; strangle. 2. To slaughter an animal without repeating the Mah. belief. (A sin). gardan hilānā , v. a. To shake one's head;…

s. f. The neck. گردن بند gardan-band, s. m. A collar, necklace. گردن زدني gardan-zadanī, adj. Deserving to be beheaded. گردن کاٿنا gardan kāṭnā, To behead. گردن کا ڐورا see under ڐورا ḍorā. گردن کش gardan-kash, adj. Proud, haughty, vain, insolent, refractory, rebellious, stubborn. گردن کشي gardan-kash-ī, s. f. Insolence, rebelliousness, arrogance. گردن کشي کرنا gardan-kashī karnā, To rebel. گردن مارنا gardan mārnā, To behead, to kill, to put to death. گردني دينا gardanī denā, To drive a man by the neck.
subs: or gardan kāṭnā, or gardan mārnā (-kī), To behead, decapitate
s.m. A chain, collar, necklace, ornament for the neck
(-kā of the blood, and -kī or -rī of the person), To have (one's) blood on the neck (or head), to be guilty of the blood (of)
(kisī-kī), 'To mount the neck (of),' to tyrannize (over), to practise violence, or extortion, &c. (upon)
(apnī), To put the neck in a noose; to involve oneself in difficulty or trouble; to incur responsibility
To bow the neck; to submit, yield
(-kī), To be beheaded; — to be ruined or undone
adj. & s.m. 'Exalting the neck'; proud, haughty, vain; insolent, refractory, rebellious, disobedient; stubborn, obstinate; — a proud person, &c.
s.f. Pride, arrogance, presumption, insolence; rebelliousness, disobedience; stubbornness; — gardan-kashī karnā, To be proud or arrogant; to rebel, &c.
(-kī), To twist (or to wring) the neck (of); to strangle, to throttle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَرْدَن (gardan). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi گردن (grdn /⁠gardan⁠/).

ety: rt. of gardānīdan, caus. of gardīdan; and = gard, q.v.+suff. ān
def: part. Causing to revolve or go round, revolving, turning; winding; changing; inverting; converting (used as last member of compounds); — s.f. Turning, going round, revolving, revolution; winding; a swimming (of the head); inflexion, declension; conjugation; revision; — adj. Trained (e.g. gardān kabūtar)
more: n. f. 1. Turning; revolving. 2. A swimming of the head. 3. ( Gram .) Inflection; conjugation; declension. 4. Revision ( daur , 6). 5. A homing bird. gardān , adj. Trained. Gardān kabūtar. gardān h. , v. n. To be trained.
s. f. Turning, revolving, revolution, winding. 2. Conjugation, declension (in grammar). 3. part. act. (from گردانيدن) Revolving, turning, causing to revolve or go round.

def: s.m. The neck ( = gardan)
subs: a blow on the nape of the neck
(-ko), To give (one) a blow on the nape of the neck.

def: s.f. The pointers (two stars in the Great Bear).
more: s. f. The pointers (two stars in the great bear).

def: direct plural of گَرْدَن (gardan)
s.f. Turning, revolving, &c. (used as last member of compounds).
more: in compos. s. f. Turning, revolving.

def: s.f. A covering for the neck (of a horse), a horse), a horse-cloth; — a blow on the nape of the neck; — a trick in wrestling; — a silver ring worn on the neck; — a cornice
more: n. f. 1. Horse-clothing. 2. A trick in wrestling. 3. A silver ring worn on the neck. 4. ( Arch .) The cornice. 5. The nape of a coat. gardanī denā, gardan pakaṛ-ke-nikāl denā , v. a. To turn one out by the scruff of the neck.
s. f. 1. A horse-cloth. 2. A blow on the neck. 3. A ring of silver worn on the neck.
subs: (-ko), To drive (a man) by the neck
(-ko), To strike (one) on the nape of the neck.

def: v.t. To turn, turn over; to inflect; to conjugate; to wrap up, roll up; to close, shut (as a book, &c.); to go over, repeat, revise (a lesson); — to regard, esteem.
more: P. H., v. a. 1. To wrap up; roll up. 2. To repeat; revise. Sabaq gardānnā . 3. To conjugate, etc. 4. To close; shut. Kitāb gardānnā . 5. To esteem; regard. Woh mujhe nahīṅ gardāntā .
v. a. To conjugate, to turn, to inflect.

def: oblique plural of گَرْدَن (gardan)
s.m.(?) Name of a tribe of Saiyids in the district of Muzaffarpūr.
s.f. Wandering, travelling; — change; revolution; — separation; downfall, misfortune, affliction (used as last member of compounds)
more: n. f. ( In compos .) Wandering; change; revolution. ashrāf-gardī . The downfall of the aristocracy. pādshāh-gardī . Revolution; anarchy. paṭhān-gardī . The Paṭhān invasion under Ahmad Shāh Durrānī.

(in compos.) s. f. 1. Change, revolution, wandering, travelling. 2. Separation, misfortune, affliction. پادشاه گردي pādshāh-gardī, The dethroning of a king. اشراف گردي ashrāf-gardī, A calamity on all the nobility. پٿهان گردي paṭhān-gardī, The invasion of the Paṭhāns under Aḥmad Shāh Durrānī.
subs: s.f. A calamity on the nobility; downfall of the aristocracy
s.f. Dethronement of a king; revolution; anarchy
s.f. The Paṭhāṇ invasion (under Aḥmad Shāh Durrānī).

ety: perf. part of gardīdan; see gard
def: part. Turned; turned away; converted; changed; estranged.
more: part. pass. (of گرديدن) Turned, converted, estranged.

def: s.f. Going round, turning round, revolution; circulation; roll; course; period; turn, change; vicissitude; reversion; — adverse fortune, adversity; — wandering about, vagrancy
more: n. f. گرديدن 1. Revolution; course. 2. Vicissitudes of fortune; adverse fortune. Gardish-i-aiyām . gardish-i-āsmān , n. m. The revolution of the spheres. gardish-i-khūn . The circulation of the blood. gardish-i-zamīn , The revolution of the earth. gardish k. , v. n. To revolve; move or go round. gardish meṅ ānā , v. n. To be at the bottom of the wheel of fortune; to be unfortunate. qismat yā dinoṅ kī gardish . The vicissitudes of fortune; hard times.
(from گرديدن) s. f. Turning round, turn, revolution, period, vicissitude, reversion, circulation, roll.
subs: To go or turn round, to revolve; to circulate (as the blood); to wander about
To be or become in a state of revolution, &c.; to fall into adversity, to become unfortunate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گردش (gardiš).

ety: S. गर्त+oṛā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: s.m. (dialec.) A small pit.

ety: gard, q.v.+ūn = wan = wān = Zend vant = S. वन्त् (nom. वान्)
def: s.m. A wheel; the heavens, the firmament, the celestial globe or sphere; chance, fortune (and her revolving wheel); — an engine (for pulling up trees by the roots, &c.), a windlass; — a chariot, chaise; a child's go-cart (by which he learns to walk).
more: s. m. 1. A wheel. 2. An engine for pulling up trees by the roots. 3. The firmament, the heavens. 4. Fortune, chance. 5. A chariot, a childʼs go-cart.

def: Poet. n. m. The firmament; the heavens.
s.m. Land mortgaged for a time, or until the loan taken is repaid by the rents.
more: n. m. Land mortgaged for a time or until the loan is repaid by the rents.

def: s.m. Circuit; round; — adv. Around, round about (e.g. jahāz ke garer dauṛ, 'running round the ship').
s.m. (dialec.) The receptacle for the sugar-cane before it is cut at the mill.
s.f. A joint or piece of the sugar-cane (the portion between two knots).
s.m. A shepherd, goat-herd.
